payload.answer: This appears to be a duplicate delivery of the same Slack event, so no durable memory change should be made unless the user confirms a distinct update is intended.
citations: Same Slack event ID delivered twice for: Sam from Goldman likes Bill Evans.
repair_options: Treat the second Slack delivery as a duplicate and ignore it.
Ask the user whether the repeated Slack event represents a new or distinct fact before saving anything.
If confirmed as distinct despite the same event ID, offer to add it as a separate memory rather than merging automatically.
